name: rr-toast
summary: Transient floating notification with status variants, optional auto-dismiss (hover/focus-paused), an action slot, and live-region semantics.

slots.name: action
slots.description: Optional action, e.g. an Undo <rr-button variant="ghost">.

events.name: rr-toast-close
events.detail: Fired when the toast closes. detail.reason is 'dismiss' | 'timeout' | 'programmatic'.

rules: Never use hex values. Always use var(--color-*) semantic tokens
Never reference --primitive-* tokens in component code
All text/background pairings must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1)
Placement is the consumer's job — render toasts inside a fixed-position region; the component owns the surface, not the position
Do not rely on the accent color alone to convey the variant — pair with a heading

examples.title: Danger, persistent
examples.html: <rr-toast variant="danger" heading="Upload failed">The file could not be processed.</rr-toast>

examples.title: With undo action
examples.html:
<rr-toast heading="Rule deleted">
  "High-value approvals" was removed.
  <rr-button slot="action" variant="ghost" size="sm">Undo</rr-button>
</rr-toast>

keyboard: Tab moves focus to the action and dismiss button
Enter or Space activates the focused control
The auto-dismiss timer pauses while the toast is hovered or holds focus
rules: role=status with aria-live=polite by default; the danger variant escalates to role=alert / assertive
The dismiss button carries an aria-label of "Dismiss"
Never put the only path to an action inside an auto-dismissing toast — duration must be 0 when an action slot is used, or the action must exist elsewhere
